error.signature: RuntimeError: DataLoader worker (pid(s) 12345) exited unexpectedly: OSError: [Errno 9] Bad file descriptor

verdict.summary: DataLoader worker process crashes with bad file descriptor. Usually caused by opening files in __init__ instead of __getitem__, or by fork-related resource sharing issues.

dead_ends.action: Set num_workers=0 as a permanent fix
dead_ends.why_fails: Eliminates multiprocessing but makes data loading the bottleneck; training throughput drops significantly
The issue is file handles not being picklable across process fork, not hitting the limit

workarounds.action: Open files lazily in __getitem__ instead of __init__
workarounds.how: Store file paths in __init__; open files in __getitem__: with open(self.paths[idx]) as f: data = f.read()

workarounds.action: Use multiprocessing start method 'spawn' instead of 'fork'
Use persistent_workers=True to keep workers alive across epochs
workarounds.how: DataLoader(dataset, num_workers=4, persistent_workers=True)  # workers keep their file handles open
